h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: the stamp only proves timing, not who had to answer
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: "received" is just the archive saying it has the paper, not the quote leaning on anyone yet.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was converging toward a clean custody-vs-use binary and appeared ready to rewrite the caption once the routing-line criterion was confirmed.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [turns the attachment slip first toward the mail tray, then toward the claims desk] In the tray, "attached" only keeps papers moving together. At the claims desk, the same slip changes status if it says "for response," names an office, or requires a reply.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L3's residual doubt about the attachment mark, T3 abandons the abstract custody/use taxonomy and instead performs a physical object-work demonstration — turning the same slip between two locations — to let the mark's context decide its own verb, a genuine representation shift from verbal sorting to spatial enactment.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: So let the mark decide its own verb. Does this record only bind the quote to another paper, or does it assign someone a duty under it?
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 directly takes up the learner's revised framing ('is circulation already use?') and reframes the question as one the mark itself must answer, shifting the epistemic standard from the learner's judgment to the document's own language — a visible adaptation to the learner's new, more granular frame.
